name: Bob Hope
abstract: Lester Townes "Bob" Hope (né Leslie Townes Hope; May 29, 1903 – July 27, 2003) was a British-born American comedian, actor, entertainer and producer with a career that spanned nearly 80 years and achievements in vaudeville, network radio, television, and USO Tours. He appeared in more than 70 short and feature films, starring in 54,  inc…

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: dad was on the radio and he was on every week and was very well known and he would work with either Bob Hope before I was born
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Bley Carla
about_quotes.evidence: my first movie that I ever saw then was Bob Hope... I was on vacation with a friend of mine, and I got to sneak in and see it
about_quotes.doc_title: Bley Carla — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I understand he's worse, he's worse than, as bad as Bob Hope almost.

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: Bob Hope had “Thanks for the Memories,” all those tunes. I used to sing them all.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: And I used to work for Bob Hope, he and his brother, Jack Hope. I used to have to go -- I used to make these -- every time they’d do a benefit or something I had to go play for them, right, keep time for them
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Noah Evans
about_quotes.evidence: I worked for Bob Hope who would rather be listed as the ten men paying the highest income tax in the United States than pay a decent wage.
